title: Podman incorrectly scopes build args when targeting a specific layer
body:
### Issue Description

On both podman 5.2.0 and 4.9.5 on MacOS, if I target a specific layer the build args I define prior to that scope are ignored, even if they define the layer itself. If I run the same script using podman 4.9.4 on Fedora it succeeds to create the image as expected.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create Containerfile:
```Containerfile
ARG FEDORA_ARCH
ARG FEDORA_VERSION

# base
FROM fedora:${FEDORA_VERSION}-${FEDORA_ARCH} as base
RUN echo "Hello from base"

# specific-project
ARG BASE_TAG
FROM base:${BASE_TAG} as project
RUN echo "Hello from base:${BASE_TAG}"
```
2. Run this script
```sh
FEDORA_VERSION=39
FEDORA_ARCH=aarch64
BASE_TAG=123

podman build \
    --build-arg "FEDORA_VERSION=$FEDORA_VERSION" \
    --build-arg "FEDORA_ARCH=$FEDORA_ARCH" \
    --target="base" \
    --tag="base:$BASE_TAG" \
    -f "Containerfile" \
    .

podman build \
    --build-arg "BASE_TAG=$BASE_TAG" \
    --target="project" \
    -f "Containerfile" \
    .
```


### Describe the results you received

```
mac-21:31:55 /tmp$ ./test.sh              
time="2024-09-11T21:31:58-04:00" level=warning msg="missing \"BASE_TAG\" build argument. Try adding \"--build-arg BASE_TAG=<VALUE>\" to the command line"
STEP 1/3: FROM fedora:39-aarch64 AS base
STEP 2/3: RUN echo "Hello from base"
Hello from base
--> 4d0c8d30fdf4
STEP 3/3: ARG BASE_TAG
COMMIT base:123
--> b9f010a031ba
Successfully tagged localhost/base:123
b9f010a031ba5c36c057baef49e8f4d8e1e5e6bfef8a9077919dd60339cdd7cb
[2/2] STEP 1/2: FROM base: AS project
Error: creating build container: parsing reference "base:": invalid reference format
```

### Describe the results you expected

The image to successfully build. Example from 4.9.4 fedora
```
WARN[0000] missing "BASE_TAG" build argument. Try adding "--build-arg BASE_TAG=<VALUE>" to the command line 
STEP 1/3: FROM fedora:39-aarch64 AS base
STEP 2/3: RUN echo "Hello from base"
Hello from base
--> 774b42237987
STEP 3/3: ARG BASE_TAG
COMMIT base:123
--> 4c47820ebd5b
Successfully tagged localhost/base:123
4c47820ebd5bc60514e66b8fc0b17df6fcc11d1171aee8f9a44ac043a4ee8ac5
[2/2] STEP 1/2: FROM base:123 AS project
[2/2] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "Hello from base:${BASE_TAG} "
Hello from base: 
[2/2] COMMIT
--> 303f45c08d8f
303f45c08d8fffbe8218e791410939a52d5db5e6e685b5d17193212a5b460619
dev-21:36:26 /tmp$ podman --version
podman version 4.9.4
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: @nalind what do you make of this ?

ARGs which are referred to in FROM instructions need to be defined in the "header", which is the area before the first FROM instruction.  In the example given, the BASE_TAG build-arg is defined but not referenced in the first stage, so it is not defined for the second stage.

> ARGs which are referred to in FROM instructions need to be defined in the "header", which is the area before the first FROM instruction. In the example given, the BASE_TAG build-arg is defined but not referenced in the first stage, so it is not defined for the second stage.

I guess that is a fair assessment. So we would says this is a bug that it works in 4.9.4 on Fedora?

Yes, it was a bug, but I thought that change would have taken effect before 4.9.4, since the bits that implement this were fixed to align better with `docker build` way back in https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/151.
